script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'harden-pass5-batch16-review',
  description: 'Adversarially review batch-16 fixes (pass-5 findings) against the working tree before commit',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Review', detail: 'one adversarial reviewer per fix group' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'aggregate verdicts' },
  ],
}

const VERDICT = {
  type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
  properties: {
    unit: { type: 'string' },
    resolves: { type: 'boolean', description: 'fix correctly resolves the finding(s)' },
    introduces_regression: { type: 'boolean' },
    confidence: { type: 'string', enum: ['low', 'medium', 'high'] },
    severity_if_problem: { type: 'string', enum: ['none', 'low', 'medium', 'high'] },
    findings: { type: 'string', description: 'what you verified in the working tree; cite file:line; state any real defect' },
    recommended_action: { type: 'string', description: 'empty if sound as-is' },
  },
  required: ['unit', 'resolves', 'introduces_regression', 'confidence', 'severity_if_problem', 'findings', 'recommended_action'],
}

const REPO = '/Users/thomasjones/hc-grc'

const UNITS = [
  { unit: '#1 timeout-pool leak on submit() failure', files: 'src/reasoning_client.py (_submit_bounded)', intent: 'Increment _inflight, then submit() inside try/except that decrements on failure (and the done_callback decrements on normal completion). Prevents a leaked slot when pool.submit raises (e.g. after shutdown).', verify: 'Confirm no double-decrement (submit-failure path vs done-callback are mutually exclusive — callback only attached after successful submit), no underflow, lock held correctly, and BaseException (not just Exception) is caught. Check the new test test_submit_bounded_decrements_on_submit_failure actually proves it.' },
  { unit: '#2 complete_json records+propagates inner complete() errors', files: 'src/reasoning_client.py (complete_json)', intent: 'Wrap the complete() call in try/except ReasoningError that records on the complete_json span and re-raises, so backend errors (RateLimit/Permanent) are NOT retried as JSON-parse failures and ARE recorded on the parent span.', verify: 'Confirm a backend error propagates on first failure (call count 1), is distinct from the _extract_json parse-failure retry path below it, and the span records it. Confirm no behavior change for the happy path or the parse-retry path.' },
  { unit: '#6 temperature retry variation at high input', files: 'src/reasoning_client.py (complete_json temp formula)', intent: 'Replace min(1.0, temp+bump) with: attempt 0 = caller temp; else bump up unless >1.0, then bump down. Guarantees distinct in-range temps even at temperature=1.0.', verify: 'Mathematically check distinctness for temperature in {0.0,0.8,0.9,1.0} across MAX_RETRIES+1 attempts; confirm all stay in [0,1] and attempt 0 == caller temp. Check the new high-input test.' },
  { unit: '#9/#10 T3 stop_reason + agent_id on spans', files: 'src/reasoning_client.py (_t3_async, complete, complete_json)', intent: 'Capture stop_reason on successful ResultMessage into meta; record as llm.finish_reason in complete(); add hcgrc.agent_id to the complete_json parent span.', verify: 'Confirm stop_reason captured only when present, finish_reason set only when present, agent_id mirrors complete()s child-span pattern, no crash when fields absent.' },
  { unit: '#7/#8 checkpointer thread-safety', files: 'src/checkpointer.py (_init_lock, _register_pool_cleanup, setup-once guard)', intent: 'Add a module threading.Lock guarding the in-process check-and-set of _atexit_registered and _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS so concurrent threads do not double-register atexit or double-run setup().', verify: 'Confirm both check-and-sets are inside the lock, the PG advisory lock still serializes cross-process, no deadlock (lock not held across unrelated blocking I/O beyond the intended setup), and setup still added to the set only after success.' },
  { unit: '#11 build_graph return annotation', files: 'src/graph.py', intent: 'Annotation changed StateGraph -> CompiledStateGraph, imported from langgraph.graph.state (verified importable there, not langgraph.graph).', verify: 'Confirm the import path is correct for the installed langgraph and matches the actual compile() return type; no runtime effect.' },
  { unit: '#15 eda_artifacts O(n+m) dedup', files: 'src/state.py', intent: 'Reducer now builds set(a) once for membership instead of x not in a per element; preserves order and dedup semantics.', verify: 'Confirm identical results to the old reducer (order preserved, dups dropped) and that the lambda is valid. Check the new reducer tests.' },
  { unit: '#3 phoenix loopback consolidation + phoenix_endpoint guard', files: 'src/infrastructure/observability/phoenix_setup.py, src/infrastructure/config.py', intent: 'phoenix_setup now imports the canonical require_loopback/LOOPBACK_HOSTS from config (removing its local copy) and phoenix_endpoint() validates loopback itself. config.require_loopback raises ConfigError (a ValueError subclass).', verify: 'Confirm exception type change ValueError->ConfigError does not break callers (bootstrap_observability catches broadly; ConfigError IS-A ValueError). Confirm launch_server_command + instrument_langchain still validate, phoenix_endpoint validates at source, and the _LOOPBACK_HOSTS alias import is fine. Check the new tests.' },
  { unit: '#4/#13 qdrant loopback guard', files: 'src/infrastructure/vector_store/qdrant_setup.py', intent: 'get_qdrant_client validates require_loopback(host) BEFORE importing qdrant_client, so the guard fires even without the package.', verify: 'Confirm guard precedes the optional import and the client construction, host passed correctly, no behavior change for loopback config. Check the new test that needs no qdrant_client.' },
  { unit: '#5/#14 mlflow loopback + config validation', files: 'src/infrastructure/tracking/mlflow_setup.py, src/infrastructure/config.py', intent: 'configure_mlflow calls require_local_uri(uri) before set_tracking_uri; config _validate_config rejects remote mlflow tracking_uri and non-loopback vector_store/phoenix/checkpointing hosts at load time.', verify: 'Confirm require_local_uri logic: sqlite:/// and bare paths pass, networked schemes require loopback host, file:// (no host) passes. Confirm config-load validation does not break the real platform.yaml (all localhost). Check the new tests.' },
  { unit: '#12 docker-compose loopback bind', files: 'docker-compose.yml', intent: 'Qdrant ports bind 127.0.0.1 only instead of 0.0.0.0.', verify: 'Confirm the compose syntax "127.0.0.1:6333:6333" is valid and matches the documented Qdrant ports; no other service exposed.' },
  { unit: 'version-bound parity', files: 'requirements-ci.txt', intent: 'psycopg floor raised to >=3.2,<4 (match prod); pytest>=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6 ceilings added so CI does not drift to pytest 9.x.', verify: 'Confirm the bounds match production majors and do not conflict with the langgraph-checkpoint / postgres pins already present; no impossible resolution.' },
  { unit: 'new test suite correctness (reasoning_client + observability)', files: 'tests/test_reasoning_client.py, tests/test_observability.py', intent: 'New tests for #1,#2,#6,#9,#274,T3-rate-event,_run_sync,sigterm,collision-warning,launch-server loopback.', verify: 'Read the new tests and confirm they genuinely exercise the intended behavior (not tautological/over-mocked), assertions are meaningful, and importorskip guards are correct. Flag any test that would pass even if the fix were reverted.' },
  { unit: 'new test suite correctness (config/checkpointer/state)', files: 'tests/test_infrastructure/test_config_validation.py, tests/test_infrastructure/test_config_and_tracking.py, tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py, tests/test_phase0/test_state_reducers.py', intent: 'New tests for loopback config validation, qdrant/phoenix/mlflow runtime guards, checkpointer setup-once + connection-failure, state reducers.', verify: 'Confirm assertions are meaningful and would fail if the corresponding fix were reverted; confirm the typing.get_type_hints reducer-extraction approach is robust; confirm Postgres-gated tests are correctly gated.' },
]

phase('Review')
const verdicts = await pipeline(
  UNITS,
  (u) => agent(
    `Adversarial reviewer, HC-GRC hardening batch 16 (pass-5 fixes). Repo ${REPO}, branch hardening/pass-5, UNCOMMITTED working-tree changes — review the working tree.\n\n` +
    `UNIT: ${u.unit}\nFILES: ${u.files}\nINTENT: ${u.intent}\nVERIFY (try to refute): ${u.verify}\n\n` +
    `Read the real code (Read/Grep/Bash read-only; you may run python -c against ${REPO}/.venv). Default to skepticism. Cite file:line. Return the verdict; set resolves=false or introduces_regression=true if you find a real problem and describe it.`,
    { label: `review:${u.unit.slice(0, 32)}`, phase: 'Review', schema: VERDICT, agentType: 'Explore' }
  )
)

phase('Synthesize')
const all = verdicts.filter(Boolean)
const problems = all.filter(v => !v.resolves || v.introduces_regression || v.severity_if_problem === 'high' || v.severity_if_problem === 'medium')
log(`${all.length} units reviewed; ${problems.length} flagged`)
return { total: all.length, problems, all }

all.findings:
Implementation verified across six critical dimensions:

1. **Double-decrement (lines 151, 164-167, 155-156)**: Confirmed mutually exclusive paths. Increment happens at line 151 inside lock. If submit() succeeds at line 163, callback is registered at line 168 and decrements later via _release. If submit() raises at line 163, exception handler at lines 164-167 decrements once inside lock, and line 168 (callback registration) is never reached. No path can execute both.

2. **Underflow (lines 137, 146-151, 164-167, 155-156)**: _inflight initialized to 0. Guard at line 146 prevents incrementing if already at capacity (>= 8). Each increment is paired with exactly one decrement via either path. No possibility of negative values.

3. **Lock correctness (lines 145-151, 165-166, 155-156)**: All mutations of _inflight are protected. Increment protected by lock at 145-151. Exception handler acquires fresh lock at 165-166. Callback _release acquires lock at 155-156. No unprotected access.

4. **BaseException coverage (line 164)**: Catches `BaseException` not just `Exception`, covering RuntimeError, KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit. Correctly re-raises after decrement at line 167. Appropriate for shutdown scenarios.

5. **Test validation**: test_submit_bounded_decrements_on_submit_failure (line 262-274 of tests/test_reasoning_client.py) correctly proves the fix. Mocks _timeout_pool with _DeadPool that raises RuntimeError on submit(). Verifies _inflight returns to 0 after exception. Existing test_submit_bounded_releases_slot_on_completion validates normal path still works. All 35 tests in test_reasoning_client.py pass.

6. **Race conditions**: Callback only registered after successful submit and lock release. No window for concurrent access to _inflight between increment and callback registration. Each access to _inflight is lock-protected. Thread-safe.

Zero defects found.

IMPLEMENTATION VERIFIED: src/reasoning_client.py:618-631 correctly wraps complete() call in try/except ReasoningError that (1) records exception on complete_json span via span.record_exception() + span.set_status() at lines 629-630, (2) re-raises at line 631 to propagate immediately without retry. 

INTENT REALIZED: (a) Backend errors propagate on first failure (call count=1) — verified by test_complete_json_does_not_retry_backend_errors at line 259 asserting calls["n"]==1; (b) Distinct from parse-retry path — backend error caught+re-raised before reaching _extract_json() at line 632; (c) Span records exception — span.record_exception(exc) + span.set_status() called at lines 629-630. (d) No regression for happy path — test_complete_json_parses_stubbed_reply passes; (e) No regression for parse-retry path — test_complete_json_exhausts_retries_then_raises passes with MAX_RETRIES+1 attempts.

CRITICAL TESTS PASS: All 35 tests in test_reasoning_client.py pass including test_complete_json_does_not_retry_backend_errors (line 245), test_complete_json_parses_stubbed_reply (line 142), test_complete_json_exhausts_retries_then_raises (line 167), test_complete_json_varies_temperature_across_retries (line 185).

EXCEPTION HIERARCHY CORRECT: RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError, and generic ReasoningError all subclass ReasoningError; except clause at line 624 catches all backend-error subtypes. Bare raise at line 631 re-raises with original traceback. Loop exits immediately via raise, no continued iterations.

all.unit: #6 temperature retry variation at high input
all.findings:
FORMULA VERIFIED CORRECT:

Implementation location: /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/reasoning_client.py lines 612-617

Code change from old formula:
  OLD: attempt_temp = temperature if attempt == 0 else min(1.0, temperature + 0.2 * attempt)
  NEW: if attempt == 0:
         attempt_temp = temperature
       else:
         bump = 0.2 * attempt
         attempt_temp = (temperature + bump if temperature + bump <= 1.0
                         else max(0.0, temperature - bump))

Verification performed with temperature in {0.0, 0.8, 0.9, 1.0} and MAX_RETRIES=2 (production default):

1. ATTEMPT 0 == CALLER TEMP: PASS
   - All cases verified: attempt[0] equals input temperature for all test cases

2. ALL TEMPERATURES IN [0.0, 1.0]: PASS
   - caller=0.0  -> [0.0, 0.2, 0.4] ✓
   - caller=0.8  -> [0.8, 1.0, 0.4] ✓
   - caller=0.9  -> [0.9, 0.7, 0.5] ✓
   - caller=1.0  -> [1.0, 0.8, 0.6] ✓

3. ALL DISTINCT TEMPERATURES: PASS
   - No duplicate temperatures within any sequence

CRITICAL FIX VERIFIED:
The new formula correctly handles the high-temperature saturation problem:
- When temp + bump <= 1.0: bump UP (as before)
- When temp + bump > 1.0: bump DOWN instead (the fix)
- This prevents saturation at 1.0 that the old min(1.0, ...) formula suffered from
- Example: temperature=1.0, old formula would give [1.0, 1.0, 1.0]; new formula gives [1.0, 0.8, 0.6]

TEST SUITE STATUS:
- New test test_complete_json_varies_temperature_with_high_input: PASS (line 226-242)
- Existing test test_complete_json_varies_temperature_across_retries: PASS (line 185-202)
- All 35 tests in test_reasoning_client.py: PASS
- No regressions detected

SUPPORTING CHANGES ALSO VERIFIED:
- Line 162-167: _submit_bounded error handling to prevent slot leaks on submit() failure (pass-5)
- Line 358-360: Capture stop_reason on successful T3 runs for OpenInference span (pass-5)
- Line 536: Record llm.finish_reason in complete() span (pass-5)
- Line 605: Record agent_id on complete_json span (pass-5)

MATHEMATICAL PROOF OF DISTINCTNESS FOR MAX_RETRIES=2:
Each sequence has 3 distinct temperatures by construction:
- High temp (0.8-1.0) sequences: bump changes direction at attempt 1, creating at least 3 distinct values
- Low temp (0.0-0.5) sequences: bumps continue upward, creating distinct values
- At temperature=1.0: [1.0, 0.8, 0.6] - completely distinct, addresses the original bug

No defects found. The implementation correctly resolves the issue and passes all tests.

Verified implementation in src/reasoning_client.py (lines 359-360, 535-536, 602-605, 618-631, 612-617):

1. stop_reason captured from ResultMessage (line 359): Uses safe getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None) pattern - returns None when field absent, condition is falsy, not captured. No AttributeError possible.

2. finish_reason set on complete() span (line 535-536): Uses meta.get("stop_reason") truthiness check - only sets llm.finish_reason attribute when stop_reason is present and truthy. Empty string edge case handled (falsy, not set).

3. agent_id on complete_json parent span (line 602-605): Mirrors complete() pattern (line 514-515) with identical conditional "if agent_id: span.set_attribute(...)". Comment documents the mirror pattern.

4. complete_json error handling (line 618-631): try/except correctly wraps complete() call to distinguish backend errors (already internally retried) from JSON parse failures. ReasoningError caught, recorded on span via record_exception(), and propagated immediately without retry.

5. Temperature boundary logic (line 612-617): When temperature + bump > 1.0, code bumps downward instead of saturating, ensuring all retries stay in [0.0, 1.0] and remain distinct.

All 35 tests pass including test_complete_records_finish_reason, test_complete_json_does_not_retry_backend_errors, test_complete_json_varies_temperature_with_high_input. Edge cases verified: absent fields, empty strings, None values, backend errors - all handled safely with no crashes.

Verified all four VERIFY criteria:

1. BOTH CHECK-AND-SETS INSIDE LOCK: 
   - _atexit_registered check-and-set (src/checkpointer.py:58-62) guarded by _init_lock
   - _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS check-and-set (src/checkpointer.py:168-176) guarded by _init_lock

2. PG ADVISORY LOCK SERIALIZES CROSS-PROCESS:
   - Lock acquired at line 171: conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", ...)
   - Lock released in finally block at line 175: conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", ...)
   - Correctly pairs even if PostgresSaver(conn).setup() at line 173 raises exception

3. NO DEADLOCK:
   - threading.Lock is not reentrant, but same thread pattern is safe: _register_pool_cleanup acquires/releases lock (line 58-62), then get_checkpointer acquires/releases lock separately (line 168-176)
   - No circular wait dependencies
   - Lock not held across unrelated I/O; blocking operations (pool.connection(), setup() DDL) are intentionally serialized as part of the setup sequence
   - Pool creation (lines 138-149) occurs before lock acquisition, avoiding expensive pool creation under lock

4. SETUP ADDED TO SET ONLY AFTER SUCCESS:
   - _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str) at line 176 occurs after PostgresSaver(conn).setup() completes (line 173)
   - If setup() raises exception, line 176 is unreachable due to exception propagation from within try-finally block
   - Exception is caught at line 178 and converted to RuntimeError, preventing caller from getting invalid cached state

File: /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/graph.py
Line 40: Import added `from langgraph.graph.state import CompiledStateGraph` — verified this is the correct and only importable path in langgraph
Line 73: Return annotation changed from `StateGraph` to `CompiledStateGraph` — old annotation was incorrect because the function returns `builder.compile(...)` (line 151), not a StateGraph builder. StateGraph instance does not have .invoke() method; CompiledStateGraph does. All test usages (invoke, get_state) are compatible with CompiledStateGraph. Runtime verification: build_graph() returns CompiledStateGraph instance with correct type signature.

VERIFICATION COMPLETE - NO DEFECTS FOUND

Implementation: src/state.py:121
  eda_artifacts: Annotated[list[str], lambda a, b: (lambda seen: a + [x for x in b if x not in seen])(set(a))]

CORRECTNESS:
✓ Lambda syntax valid and correctly integrated with LangGraph reducers
✓ Deduplication works: elements from b appearing in a are filtered out
✓ Order preserved: all of a precedes all new elements from b
✓ Handles edge cases: empty lists, complete overlap, no overlap, single elements
✓ No variable capture issues or closure problems

COMPLEXITY:
✓ O(n+m) achieved via set(a) for O(1) membership testing
✓ Benchmarked: 1647x faster than old O(n*m) approach on 10k+5k elements
✓ Practical test: 0.0002s vs 0.3966s for large lists

SEMANTICS:
✓ Idempotent: re-running same reducer produces same result
✓ Associative: sequential merges == batch merges (required for map-reduce)
✓ Matches docstring intent (line 118): prevents Gate-2 reject loops from accumulating duplicates

TESTS:
✓ All 9 tests in tests/test_phase0/test_state_reducers.py pass (66.7% coverage on reducer tests)
✓ test_eda_artifacts_reducer_dedups: PASS
✓ test_eda_artifacts_reducer_preserves_order: PASS
✓ Additional comprehensive manual tests verify realistic Phase 1 scenarios

INTEGRATION:
✓ Module loads without errors
✓ Field extractable from HCGRCState via typing.get_type_hints
✓ Consistent with other reducers (gate_status, hypothesis_set, etc.)
✓ Used correctly in src/nodes/phase1.py (agents extend all_eda_artifacts)

Verified consolidation of loopback validation from phoenix_setup.py to config.py as the canonical validator. ConfigError (ValueError subclass) is properly raised by require_loopback() (config.py:40) and require_local_uri() (config.py:56). Exception type change from ValueError → ConfigError does NOT break callers: (1) ConfigError IS-A ValueError, so backward-compatible; (2) bootstrap_observability() catches broad except Exception: (phoenix_setup.py:194), so ConfigError is caught and degraded gracefully; (3) no code explicitly catches ValueError for loopback validation. All six validation points are protected: config load time (_validate_config, config.py:91-100), phoenix_endpoint (line 71), launch_server_command (line 82), instrument_langchain (line 106), get_qdrant_client (qdrant_setup.py:46), configure_mlflow (mlflow_setup.py:76). New tests verify config.ConfigError is raised at each point. _LOOPBACK_HOSTS alias preserved for backward compatibility (phoenix_setup.py:42 with noqa comment). Safe defaults to "localhost" everywhere (cfg.get("host", "localhost") pattern used in all three functions). URI validation logic correctly handles sqlite, file://, networked schemes.

VERIFIED CORRECT. Implementation in src/infrastructure/vector_store/qdrant_setup.py:46-47 correctly places require_loopback() validation BEFORE the optional qdrant_client import. Validated host is properly passed to both QdrantClient constructors (lines 51, 55). Guard fires for non-loopback hosts without needing qdrant_client installed. New test test_get_qdrant_client_rejects_non_loopback_host monkeypatches _cfg and confirms ConfigError is raised. All 8 tests pass. No regressions. Implementation matches Phoenix (phoenix_setup.py:71,82) and MLflow (mlflow_setup.py:76) guards, all using shared require_loopback/require_local_uri validators. Comments accurately describe ADR-0002 data sovereignty, point-of-use defense-in-depth, and pass-5 coordination.

VERIFICATION COMPLETE: Implementation correctly enforces ADR-0002 data-sovereignty across all three verification points.

**Requirement 1 (require_local_uri logic):**
- sqlite:// and bare paths: ✓ Pass (src/infrastructure/config.py:51-52)
- file:// (no host): ✓ Pass (src/infrastructure/config.py:53-54)
- Networked schemes (http/https/postgresql/mysql/mssql) + loopback host: ✓ Pass (src/infrastructure/config.py:53-60)
- Networked schemes + remote host: ✓ Rejected (src/infrastructure/config.py:55-59)

Tested edge cases all pass: IPv6 [::1], user:pass@localhost:port, file://localhost, case-insensitive hostname (LOCALHOST), 0.0.0.0 rejection.

**Requirement 2 (config validation at load time):**
- Real platform.yaml: ✓ Loads and validates successfully
  - MLflow tracking_uri: sqlite:///mlflow.db ✓
  - Vector store host: localhost ✓
  - Phoenix host: localhost ✓
  - Checkpointing host: localhost ✓
- _validate_config enforces loopback at lines 91, 92-93, 97, 100
- All hosts verified at load time before any runtime use

**Requirement 3 (defense-in-depth):**
- Load-time validation (config.py:87-100): ✓ Present and mandatory
- Point-of-use validation (mlflow_setup.py:76): ✓ Present and independent
- Both guards checked; configure_mlflow rejects remote URIs even if config validation was somehow bypassed

**Test Results:**
- test_config_validation.py: 21/21 PASS
- test_config_and_tracking.py: 8/8 PASS
- Full test suite: 153/153 PASS
- No regressions introduced

**Implementation Details Verified:**
- require_loopback function (config.py:36-44): Correct logic with clear error messages
- require_local_uri function (config.py:47-60): Correct URI parsing with urlparse.hostname
- LOOPBACK_HOSTS set (config.py:33): Contains localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1
- All infrastructure modules have guards: MLflow, Phoenix, Qdrant
- String conversion with str() applied to all config values before validation

Change verified as sound. Docker Compose syntax "127.0.0.1:6333:6333" and "127.0.0.1:6334:6334" is valid and correctly binds to loopback only. Ports 6333 (HTTP/REST) and 6334 (gRPC) match documented Qdrant defaults (docs/architecture/INFRASTRUCTURE.md:16, src/infrastructure/vector_store/qdrant_setup.py:25-26,52,55, configs/platform.yaml:169-170). Single service exposed; no overrides or competing compose files exist. Change implements ADR-0002 data sovereignty: without the explicit 127.0.0.1 prefix, Docker Compose binds 0.0.0.0 by default, exposing SCF-derived embeddings off-box. The loopback guard at src/infrastructure/config.py:28-44 is the canonical validator used identically across all infrastructure modules (Phoenix, Qdrant, MLflow). Platform config specifies host: localhost which passes the guard. No regressions: tests/test_infrastructure/test_config_validation.py::test_validate_rejects_non_loopback_vector_store_host PASSED (all 3 parameterized cases).

VERIFICATION SUMMARY: All version-bound parity changes in requirements-ci.txt are correct and resolve the stated intent.

CHANGES REVIEWED:
1. psycopg[binary,pool]: >=3.1,<4 → >=3.2,<4 (floor bump)
2. pytest: >=8.2.2 → >=8.2.2,<9 (ceiling added)
3. pytest-cov: >=5.0.0 → >=5.0.0,<6 (ceiling added)

CORRECTNESS FINDINGS:

✓ Psycopg floor alignment with production (file:18)
  • Production (requirements.txt:67) specifies: psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2
  • CI updated to: psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4
  • Parity confirmed: Floor matches production exactly
  • Comment accuracy (lines 14-17): Correct—states floor matches prod >=3.2

✓ Pytest ceiling prevents major-version drift (file:30)
  • Production (requirements.txt:95) pins: pytest==8.2.2 (exact)
  • CI updated to: pytest>=8.2.2,<9
  • Parity confirmed: Floor matches production pin; ceiling prevents 9.x adoption
  • Comment accuracy (lines 27-29): Correct—explains ceiling prevents drift to 9.x
  • Addresses issue #264 pattern as stated

✓ Pytest-cov ceiling prevents major-version drift (file:31)
  • Production (requirements.txt:96) pins: pytest-cov==5.0.0 (exact)
  • CI updated to: pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6
  • Parity confirmed: Floor matches production pin; ceiling prevents 6.x adoption
  • Comment accuracy (lines 27-29): Correct—explains ceiling prevents drift to 6.x
  • Addresses issue #264 pattern as stated

✓ Constraint resolution feasibility
  • All constraint ranges are non-empty and sensible (no inverted ranges)
  • langgraph-checkpoint-postgres >=3.1,<4 is compatible with psycopg >=3.2,<4
  • pytest >=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov >=5.0.0,<6 have no known incompatibilities
  • pip can deterministically resolve a valid version combination
  • No impossible resolution detected

✓ Langgraph ecosystem consistency
  • langgraph >=1.2,<2 remains unchanged and compatible
  • langgraph-checkpoint >=4,<5 (pinned by checkpoint-postgres) is compatible
  • langgraph-checkpoint-postgres >=3.1,<4 remains unchanged
  • No conflicts between postgres saver 3.1+ and psycopg 3.2+

MINOR DESIGN NOTE (not a defect):
  • pyproject.toml [dev] has uncapped constraints: pytest>=8.2.2, pytest-cov>=5.0.0
  • This differs from requirements-ci.txt which adds <9 and <6 ceilings
  • However, GitHub Actions workflow correctly uses "pip install -r requirements-ci.txt" (not -e .[dev])
  • CI is therefore NOT at risk of accidental major-version upgrades
  • This is a documented design pattern: CI uses strict requirements files; dev extras allow flexibility
all.recommended_action: None. The changes are correct and ready to commit. All bounds match production majors, ceilings prevent documented drift risks (#264 pattern), and no impossible resolutions exist.

all.findings:
All 65 tests pass (35 reasoning_client + 9 observability + 21 config_validation). Reviewed new pass-5 tests:

NEW REASONING_CLIENT TESTS (8):
- test_complete_json_varies_temperature_with_high_input (line 226): Verifies downward-bump when temp approaches 1.0. Would fail if lines 612-617 removed (test validates distinct temps: assertion at line 241).
- test_complete_json_does_not_retry_backend_errors (line 245): Verifies RateLimitError propagates immediately. Would fail without lines 624-631 (test at line 259 asserts calls["n"]==1; without fix would be 3 retries).
- test_submit_bounded_decrements_on_submit_failure (line 262): Verifies pool slot restored on submit() exception. Would fail without lines 162-167 (test at line 274 asserts _inflight==0; without fix would be 1).
- test_run_sync_no_running_loop / test_run_sync_with_running_loop (lines 277-289): Branch coverage for asyncio.get_running_loop() paths.
- test_t3_detects_rate_limit_event (line 292): Verifies RateLimitEvent classification (lines 342-344). importorskip guard correct.
- test_t3_sets_dontask_permission_mode (line 309): Verifies permission_mode="dontAsk" (line 323). importorskip guard correct.
- test_complete_records_finish_reason (line 358): Verifies stop_reason recorded as llm.finish_reason (lines 535-536).

NEW OBSERVABILITY TESTS (5):
- test_launch_server_command_rejects_non_loopback (line 45): Verifies 0.0.0.0 rejected (line 82 calls require_loopback).
- test_launch_server_command_accepts_loopback (line 52): Positive case for 127.0.0.1.
- test_install_sigterm_flush_skips_on_non_main_thread (line 60): Verifies signal.signal NOT called off-thread (line 153).
- test_install_sigterm_flush_registers_on_main_thread (line 70): Verifies signal handler registered on main (line 165).
- test_instrument_langchain_warns_on_competing_provider (line 88): Verifies warning on provider collision (lines 127-133). importorskip guards correct.

All assertions are meaningful and non-tautological. Temperature logic verified: attempt 0 gets temp=1.0, attempt 1 gets temp=0.8, attempt 2 gets temp=0.6 (all distinct, in-range). Tests would catch regressions: removing exception handlers would cause assertion failures at specific lines (259, 274). No over-mocking detected.

VERIFIED: All tests exercise meaningful assertions that would fail if fixes were reverted:

CONFIG VALIDATION (test_config_validation.py:43-95):
- test_valid_loopback_config_passes (line 43): Confirms ADR-0002 validation accepts localhost/127.0.0.1/::1
- test_validate_rejects_non_loopback_vector_store_host (lines 47-52): Confirms src/infrastructure/config.py:91 guard rejects 0.0.0.0, remote IPs, domains
- test_validate_rejects_non_loopback_phoenix_host (lines 55-59): Confirms src/infrastructure/config.py:97 guard rejects non-loopback Phoenix hosts
- test_validate_rejects_remote_mlflow_uri (lines 62-71): Confirms src/infrastructure/config.py:92-94 guard rejects remote tracking servers (http://, postgresql://)
- test_validate_accepts_local_mlflow_uri (lines 74-83): Confirms sqlite:///, relative paths, localhost:port are accepted
- test_require_loopback tests (lines 86-94): Confirm require_loopback() function rejects all non-loopback addresses

RUNTIME LOOPBACK GUARDS (test_config_and_tracking.py:64-92):
- test_get_qdrant_client_rejects_non_loopback_host (line 64): Confirms src/infrastructure/vector_store/qdrant_setup.py:46 guard fires BEFORE importing qdrant_client
- test_phoenix_endpoint_rejects_non_loopback_host (line 75): Confirms src/infrastructure/observability/phoenix_setup.py:71 guard in public API
- test_configure_mlflow_rejects_remote_uri (line 84): Confirms src/infrastructure/tracking/mlflow_setup.py:76 guard at set_tracking_uri() call site

STATE REDUCERS (test_state_reducers.py:17-72):
- typing.get_type_hints(HCGRCState, include_extras=True) + typing.get_args() extraction approach is robust (verified for all 9 Annotated fields)
- test_merge_hypotheses_* (lines 27-48): Exercises _merge_hypotheses() dedup logic (src/state.py:29-44) with 5 test cases covering distinct, dedups, position preservation, batching invariant
- test_eda_artifacts_reducer_* (lines 54-61): Confirms dedup + order preservation for src/state.py:121 lambda
- test_gate_status_reducer_* (lines 64-71): Confirms dict merge behavior for src/state.py:82 lambda

CHECKPOINTER SETUP-ONCE GUARD (test_postgres_checkpointer.py:91-124):
- test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str (line 91): Confirms src/checkpointer.py:168-176 guard deduplicates setup() calls by conn_str (monkeypatches PostgresSaver.setup to count invocations, asserts count==1 after 2 calls)
- test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure (line 113): Confirms src/checkpointer.py:178-183 error handling path surfaces operator-facing RuntimeError with "is Postgres running" guidance (monkeypatches psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool)

TEST GATING:
- test_postgres_checkpointer_round_trips, test_checkpoint_recovers_across_a_fresh_checkpointer, test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str all call _require_postgres() (lines 62, 76, 94) to skip locally when Postgres absent but FAIL in CI
- test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure (line 113) is NOT guarded with _require_postgres() but comment says "needs no real database"; the monkeypatch works when run in normal test order (prior tests load psycopg_pool), but would fail in isolation due to langgraph import timing
all.recommended_action: MINOR: Add _require_postgres() call at line 113 of tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py to ensure test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure is not fragile/order-dependent. Currently it passes only because earlier tests load psycopg_pool before monkeypatch occurs. The test should either (1) require Postgres to be present OR (2) use a monkeypatch strategy that applies before langgraph's _internal module imports ConnectionPool. Suggested fix: add `_require_postgres()` call at start of test function (line 114, after def line), OR move the monkeypatch to patch at module import level with `sys.modules` manipulation.
